page.title: diabetic neuropathy — Disease Intelligence | OSMF
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for diabetic neuropathy: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for diabetic neuropathy: 109 alterations and 186 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Curcumin may provide great potential effects against diabetic kidney disease.
Proprioceptive exercise may improve functional capacity, anxiety, and depression in diabetic neuropathy patients.
Reduced serum levels of folate and vitamin B12 were found in Chinese patients with T2DM and diabetic peripheral neuropathy.
7,8-dihydroxyflavone protects high glucose-damaged neuronal cells against oxidative stress.
Alpha lipoic has significant therapeutic value in the treatment of diabetic neuropathy.
Application of a topical formulation of C. colocynthis fruit extract can decrease pain in patients with PDPN.
Aromatherapy massage was beneficial for neuropathic pain and quality of life in diabetic patients.
Centella asiatica triterpenes are beneficial for diabetic neuropathy.
Effect of auricular acupressure on neuropathy symptoms and quality of life in patients with type 2 diabetes.
Effect of modified laser transcutaneous irradiation on pain and quality of life in patients with diabetic neuropathy.
Effect of α-lipoic acid on symptoms and quality of life in patients with painful diabetic neuropathy.
Effectiveness of photon stimulation on pain, sensation, and quality of life in patients with diabetic peripheral neuropathy.
Effects of photobiomodulation with two wavelengths of 630 and 810 nm on diabetic neuropathy.
Efficacy and safety of the combination of superoxide dismutase, alpha lipoic acid, vitamin B12, and carnitine for 12 months in patients with diabetic neuropathy.
Efficacy of low level laser therapy on painful diabetic peripheral neuropathy.
Green tea extract intake may have a beneficial value in treatment of diabetic peripheral neuropathy.
Impact of vitamin B6 deficiency on the severity of diabetic peripheral neuropathy.
Integrative therapies such as mindfulness meditation can be part of a comprehensive pain management plan.
LLLT improved the QL and hence may be a useful therapeutic option in treating peripheral neuropathic pain in type 2 diabetic patients.
N-acetylcysteine may alleviate painful symptoms of diabetic neuropathy.
Oral 600mg alpha-lipoic acid twice daily treatment for diabetic peripheral neuropathy over 6 months is effective, safe and tolerable.
Reversal of diabetic peripheral neuropathy with phototherapy (MIRE) decreases falls and the fear of falling and improves activities of daily living in seniors.
The restorative effects of pulsed infrared light therapy on significant loss of peripheral protective sensation in patients with long-term type 1 and type 2 diabetes mellitus.
This trial points towards the beneficial effect of yogic breathing program in preventing progression of cardiac neuropathy.
Topical Nigella sativa L. product: a new candidate for the management of diabetic peripheral neuropathy.
Vitamin D supplementations improve peripheral diabetic neuropathy.
Vitamin-E was found to be effective in reducing pain score in diabetic neuropathy patients.
Whole body vibration improves symptoms of diabetic peripheral neuropathy.
Influence of low-level laser on pain and inflammation in type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic dermopathy.
Antinociceptive effect of chrysin in diabetic neuropathy and formalin-induced pain models.
A ketogenic diet reduces mechanical allodynia and improves epidermal innervation in diabetic mice.
Alpha lipoic acid decreases neuronal damage on brain tissue of STZ-induced diabetic rats.
Alpha-lipoic acid has a protective effect on peripheral neuronal cell damage generated with diabetic neuropathy.
Ameliorative effect of berberine on neonatally induced type 2 diabetic neuropathy.
Analysis showed that naringenin corrected the altered sciatic changes in diabetic animals.
Anti-hyperalgesic effects of photobiomodulation therapy (904 nm) on streptozotocin-induced diabetic neuropathy.
Antihypernociceptive and neuroprotective effects of Combretin A and Combretin B on streptozotocin-induced diabetic neuropathy.
Arctigenin improves neuropathy.
B vitamins alleviate indices of neuropathic pain in diabetic rats.
Berberine ameliorates the neurological dysfunction of the gastric fundus.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Progressive with worsening glycaemic control; reversal essentially not possible with current treatments once established; spontaneous improvement only in cases of rapid glycaemic normalisation early in course
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Intensive glycaemic control in T1DM (DCCT): 60% reduction in neuropathy development; pain management (duloxetine, pregabalin): 30-50% pain reduction but no disease modification; alpha-lipoic acid IV: nerve conduction velocity improvement at 3 weeks (SYDNEY 2 trial); SGLT2 inhibitors: emerging data on neuropathy protection
remission.gap_size: Very large — no disease-modifying therapy approved; pain treatment is the only intervention most patients receive
remission.barrier_detail: Prevention through glycaemic control is highly effective but prevention means tight glycaemic control for decades; pain treatment gives symptomatic relief only; opioid prescribing for DPN pain remains common despite guidelines against it; few patients are offered alpha-lipoic acid which has the strongest disease-modification signal
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — FDA approved TENS device (Quell 2.0) for DPN pain; 2022 DPNP guidance from ADA updated; duloxetine and pregabalin as first-line since 2004-2005; no paradigm change for 20 years
remission.outdated_treatments: Tricyclic antidepressants (amitriptyline) as first-line despite equivalent efficacy and better tolerability of SNRIs in elderly; opioids for DPN (long-term opioid therapy for DPN pain — no superiority to combination non-opioid therapy; guidelines explicitly discourage); vitamin B12 monitoring not performed despite metformin-induced B12 depletion in 5-40% of T2DM patients worsening neuropathy
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Alpha-lipoic acid 600mg/day (ALA — most studied supplement for DPN; IV form with best evidence but oral showing benefit in longer studies); benfotiamine (lipid-soluble B1 — BEDIP trial positive); transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation (TENS); near-infrared phototherapy; corneal confocal microscopy for early diagnosis enabling earlier intervention
remission.frontier_signal: Aldose reductase inhibitors (epalrestat — approved in Asia, rejected FDA; Phase 3 ongoing globally); PARP inhibitors; BDNF analogs for nerve regeneration; sodium channel subtype-specific blockers (Nav1.7, Nav1.8) for pain without systemic effects; stem cell-based nerve regeneration for irreversible cases
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
